comments.text: Don't try kayaking from Madeline Island to Michigan Island even if you think it's "close".  People have died on afternoon pleasure trips, underestimating the changeability and ferocity of Lake Superior.

comments.author: @sheilacollins9384
comments.text:
I love Door County in Wisconsin.  It's a thin peninsula that's got Green Bay on one side and Lake Michigan on the other.  Little video of Cana Island in this documentary.  It's near Baileys Harbor and waves from Lake Michigan criss cross each other to make a strange watery checkerboard that you can see from the top of the lighthouse.  Some summers you can wade out to the island.
There is a piece about Door County that is incorrect here.  The most incredible  cliffs (and I jump from them each summer) are on the Michigan side, not the Bay.  The video is from Cave Point, which is close to Whitefish Dunes, but not the same landscape.  One is beautiful cliff & caves, one is a beautiful beach.  You can kayak from Jacksonport to Whitefish.  It's lovely - and it's all Lake Michigan.

replies.author: @user-ep5sp7kr4l
replies.text:
​​@denniss5505nonsence....... 

Lake Baikal : 23,600km3
All 5 Great Lakes : 22,700km3

I rest my case......I mean case closed.

Technical reasoning : great lakes have a bigger surface area, but lake Baikal Is the deepest body of fresh water on earth in excess of 1600m at its deepest point

replies.author: @denniss5505
replies.text: Lake Baykal holds 19-20% of earths fresh water whereas the Great Lakes hold 21-22% so yes Baykal being one lake is larger but, the Great Lakes hold more water, and if we’re being technical, Lake Michigan and Huron is one body of water making it similar in size to the Baykal which freezes completely until June. Though the Great Lakes freeze, they are never frozen over only a couple miles or so along the shoreline

replies.author: @keithbarto5464
replies.text:
I was just on a few nights stay last week at a friends camp on the south shores of Shelter Bay between  Marquette and Munising.  I am not religious-but if there were any such thing as “gods country”-

Lake Superior and the UP along the Gitchi Gumee southern shoreline— IS IT.  

I didnt get to see Keeweenaw this trip but Im a deep south native who’s heart was stolen last week, by the NORTH . 

I was heartbroken to leave , and I am forever changed by my experience on the Great Sea and the wilderness that I will be the closest I want to ever get to see what any posthumous “heaven” could never compare to .
